questions.question: In a two-way table, what does a cell represent?
A two-way table shows the relationship between $\text{Temperature}$ and $\text{Rainfall}$. If the table has 3 rows and 4 columns, how many cells are there?
What type of frequency is calculated by dividing a cell frequency by the total frequency?
In a study of the relationship between $\text{Ecosystem}$ and $\text{Species}$, which type of table would be used?
If a two-way table has 2 rows and 3 columns, and the total frequency is $24$, what is the relative frequency of the cell in the first row and first column if its joint frequency is $6$?

A two-way table shows the relationship between $\text{Weather}$ and $\text{Tourist Activity}$. If the relative frequency of sunny weather is $0.4$ and the relative frequency of tourist activity is $0.6$, what is the relative frequency of sunny weather and tourist activity together if they are independent?
In a two-way table, what does the marginal frequency of a row represent?

options.A: The total frequency of the row
options.B: The relative frequency of the row
options.C: The joint frequency of the row
options.D: The conditional frequency of the row
options.E: The total frequency of the table

questions.question: A two-way table shows the relationship between $\text{Temperature Change}$ and $\text{Ecosystem}$. If there is a strong positive association between the two variables, what can be concluded?

options.A: As temperature change increases, ecosystem decreases
options.B: As temperature change increases, ecosystem increases
options.C: As temperature change decreases, ecosystem decreases
options.D: As temperature change decreases, ecosystem increases
options.E: There is no relationship between temperature change and ecosystem

questions.question:
A two-way table shows the relationship between $\text{Rainfall}$ and $\text{Crop Yield}$. The table is as follows: 
$\begin{array}{c|c|c|c} & \text{Low} & \text{Medium} & \text{High} \\ \hline \text{Low} & 10 & 15 & 20 \\ \text{Medium} & 20 & 30 & 10 \\ \text{High} & 30 & 20 & 15 \\ \end{array}$
 Calculate the relative frequency of low rainfall and low crop yield. Show your work and give your answer as a decimal value.

A study examines the relationship between $\text{Weather Conditions}$ and $\text{Air Quality}$. The two-way table is as follows: 
$\begin{array}{c|c|c|c} & \text{Good} & \text{Fair} & \text{Poor} \\ \hline \text{Sunny} & 25 & 30 & 10 \\ \text{Cloudy} & 30 & 20 & 15 \\ \text{Rainy} & 10 & 25 & 20 \\ \end{array}$
 Analyze the association between weather conditions and air quality. Provide a conclusion based on the data and give at least two pieces of evidence from the table to support your conclusion.

teacher_rules: Tip 1: Make sure to read each question carefully and understand what is being asked.
Tip 2: Use the data in the two-way tables to calculate relative frequencies and analyze associations between variables.
Tip 3: Show all work and provide clear explanations for free-response questions.
